steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows 'it is not surprising that' and must complete a logical consequence of gold being 'much rarer than bronze.' Whatever goes in the blank should follow naturally from scarcity: if gold was rare, not every hoard would contain it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "some hoards contained no gold, as was the case for the Parc-y-Meirch hoard.": if gold was rare, it makes sense that some hoards had none at all — the Parc-y-Meirch hoard is a named example from the passage. ✓
  • "most Bronze Age gold objects were melted down so that the gold could be reused.": the passage says nothing about melting or reuse; this is unsupported. ✗
  • "the skills needed to make tools and weapons from bronze were easy to adapt to making decorative items from gold.": the passage does not discuss skill transfer between metals; this is irrelevant to rarity. ✗
  • "some hoards were found as a result of artifacts being dug up by accident.": discovery method has nothing to do with gold's rarity relative to bronze. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
